channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Vance slams Denmark's handling of Greenland security
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday slammed Denmark as failing to prioritize Greenland’s security, escalating rhetoric between Washington and Copenhagen amid President Trump’s claims to take over the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: UN chief: World leaders must speak with one voice to prevent a new civil war in South Sudan
item.description: The United Nations chief is urging regional and international leaders to prevent South Sudan from falling “over the abyss” into another civil war, and to speak with one voice to support a return to peace.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Powerful quake rocks Myanmar and Thailand and kills more than 150 people
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A powerful earthquake rocked Myanmar and neighboring Thailand on Friday, destroying buildings, bridges and a monastery. At least 144 people were killed in Myanmar, where photos and video from two hard-hit cities showed extensive damage.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US and Lithuanian forces struggle to drain swamp to recover 4 soldiers in submerged armored vehicle
item.description: U.S. and Lithuanian troops and authorities are struggling to drain a swampy area in Lithuania in ongoing efforts to recover four American Army soldiers who went missing three days ago and are warning that it will be a long operation.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump holds 'productive call' with new Canadian prime minister
item.description: President Trump spoke with Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Friday for the first time since Carney was elected earlier in the month amid tensions between the two nations.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: What to know about the US military's Pituffik Space Base in Greenland
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — The remote Pituffik Space Base in northwestern Greenland, which Vice President JD Vance is visiting on Friday, is the U.S. Department of Defense's northernmost installation. The base was built following a 1951 defense agreement between Denmark and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea's worst wildfires are now almost contained following rain and cooler weather
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The most destructive wildfires ever to hit South Korea were almost contained, authorities announced Friday, after rain and cooler temperatures helped fire crew put out the blazes that have killed 28 people and razed vast swaths of land since last week.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 150
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Putin suggests putting Ukraine under UN-sponsored external governance
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has proposed putting Ukraine under external governance under the auspices of the U.N. as part of efforts to reach a peaceful settlement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Powerful quake rocks Myanmar and Thailand and kills more than 150 people
item.description: A powerful earthquake has rocked Myanmar and neighboring Thailand, destroying buildings and bridges and killing at least 150 people, mostly in Myanmar.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Suspected US strikes pummel Houthi-controlled areas of Yemen
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pummeled sites across Yemen controlled by the Houthi rebels early Friday, including neighborhoods in the capital, Sanaa.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Lithuanian, Polish troops aid in 'search and recovery' for 4 missing US soldiers from Fort Stewart
item.description: A Lithuanian official told Fox News Digital about ongoing search efforts to recover four missing U.S. Army soldiers in coordination with Poland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Knife-wielding man injures multiple people in Amsterdam, including 2 Americans
item.description: Dutch police are searching for a motive after five people, including two Americans, were stabbed in a busy shopping area of Amsterdam on Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A stabbing attack in Amsterdam wounds five people, including four foreigners
item.description: AMSTERDAM (AP) — A knife-wielding assailant in Amsterdam seriously wounded five people — including two from the United States, one from Belgium and one from Poland — in a stabbing attack Thursday on a busy shopping street, Dutch police said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-President Martinelli gets Panama's permission to take asylum in Nicaragua
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Ex-Panama President Ricardo Martinelli will be allowed to leave the Nicaraguan embassy where he’s been holed up for more than a year to travel to Nicaragua for humanitarian reasons, Panama Foreign Affairs Minister Javier Martínez-Acha Vásquez said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US-Canada airline bookings plummet 70 percent: Report
item.description: Airline bookings between the U.S. and Canada are down by 70 percent compared to the same time last year, according to the aviation analytics company OAG.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Crowds flock to newest Catholic saint in Assisi
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of pilgrims have been pouring into the medieval hilltop town of Assisi in Italy to venerate the Catholic Church’s newest saint.
item.dc:creator: Giovanna Dell'Orto, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard, Ratcliffe, Patel criticized over Signal chat at hearing
item.description: Though the hearing is about the 2025 Annual Worldwide Threats Assessment, officials were questioned about a Signal group chat leak.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: French actor Gérard Depardieu stands trial in Paris for alleged sexual assaults on film set
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French actor Gérard Depardieu went on trial Monday in Paris on charges of sexually assaulting two women on a movie set, in a case seen as a potential watershed for the #MeToo movement in France.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill scores in Gaza as Egypt offers new ceasefire proposal
item.description: Israeli strikes across the Gaza Strip killed more than 65 Palestinians over the past day, including women and children, the Gaza Health Ministry said Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A Russian drone attack kills 7 in Ukraine ahead of ceasefire talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched a barrage of drones across Ukraine overnight Sunday that killed at least seven people, according to local Ukrainian officials and emergency services.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: America's European allies try to pry unspent money back from USAID
item.description: Three European allies provided millions of dollars that the United States was supposed to spend for low-income countries.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, JAMEY KEATEN, MIKE CORDER AND VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Oleg Gordievsky, Britain's most valuable Cold War spy inside the KGB, dies at 86
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Oleg Gordievsky, a Soviet KGB officer who helped change the course of the Cold War by covertly passing secrets to Britain, has died. He was 86. Gordievsky died March 4 in England, where he had lived since defecting in 1985.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation
